fullOutput:
<path>/var/repos/oxIDIZER/scripts/run_systems_tests.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # Default values
4: SCRIPT_DIR=$(dirname "$(readlink -f "$0")")
5: DEFAULT_SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR"
6: DEFAULT_TEST_LIBS_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")/tests/common"
7: DEFAULT_RUNNING_MODE="isolated"
8: DEFAULT_LOGGING_LEVEL="info"
9: DEFAULT_TARGET="debug"
10: DEFAULT_PORT_START=3000
11: DEFAULT_PORT_END=3099
12: MODULE_FILE=""
13: 
14: # Source the logging function
15: source "$DEFAULT_TEST_LIBS_DIR/log_function.sh"
16: 
17: # --- Function to display usage ---
18: usage() {
19:     echo "Usage: $0 -f <module_file> [options]"
20:     echo "Options:"
21:     echo "  -f, --file <file>          Specify the file containing the list of modules to test (one per line)."
22:     echo "  -s, --scripts-dir <dir>      Specify the support scripts directory (default: $DEFAULT_SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR)"
23:     echo "  -t, --test-libs-dir <dir>    Specify the common test libraries directory (default: $DEFAULT_TEST_LIBS_DIR)"
24:     echo "  -m, --mode <mode>            Specify the running mode (default: $DEFAULT_RUNNING_MODE)"
25:     echo "  -l, --log-level <level>      Specify the logging level (default: $DEFAULT_LOGGING_LEVEL)"
26:     echo "  --port-start <port>          Start of port range (default: $DEFAULT_PORT_START)"
27:     echo "  --port-end <port>            End of port range (default: $DEFAULT_PORT_END)"
28:     echo "  --build                      Pre-build the workspace before running (default: false)"
29:     echo "  -h, --help                   Display this help message"
30: }
31: 
32: # --- Parse command-line options ---
33: SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR=$DEFAULT_SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR
34: TEST_LIBS_DIR=$DEFAULT_TEST_LIBS_DIR
35: RUNNING_MODE=$DEFAULT_RUNNING_MODE
36: LOGGING_LEVEL=$DEFAULT_LOGGING_LEVEL
37: TARGET=$DEFAULT_TARGET
38: PORT_START=$DEFAULT_PORT_START
39: PORT_END=$DEFAULT_PORT_END
40: 
41: # Use getopt to parse arguments
42: TEMP=$(getopt -o f:s:t:m:l:h --long file:,scripts-dir:,test-libs-dir:,mode:,log-level:,help,port-start:,port-end:,build -n 'run_systems_tests.sh' -- "$@")
43: 
44: if [ $? != 0 ]; then
45:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Terminating..."
46:     exit 1
47: fi
48: 
49:  eval set -- "$TEMP"
50: 
51: while true; do
52:     case "$1" in
53:         -f | --file)
54:             MODULE_FILE="$2"
55:             shift 2
56:             ;;
57:         -s | --scripts-dir)
58:             SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR="$2"
59:             shift 2
60:             ;;
61:         -t | --test-libs-dir)
62:             TEST_LIBS_DIR="$2"
63:             shift 2
64:             ;;
65:         -m | --mode)
66:             RUNNING_MODE="$2"
67:             shift 2
68:             ;;
69:         -l | --log-level)
70:             LOGGING_LEVEL="$2"
71:             shift 2
72:             ;;
73:         --port-start)
74:             PORT_START="$2"
75:             shift 2
76:             ;;
77:         --port-end)
78:             PORT_END="$2"
79:             shift 2
80:             ;;
81:         --build)
82:             DO_BUILD="true"
83:             shift
84:             ;;
85:         -h | --help)
86:             usage
87:             exit 0
88:             ;;
89:         --)
90:             shift
91:             break
92:             ;;
93:         *)
94:             log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Internal error!"
95:             exit 1
96:             ;;
97:     esac
98: done
99: 
100: # Check if a module file was provided
101: if [ -z "$MODULE_FILE" ]; then
102:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "No module file specified."
103:     usage
104:     exit 1
105: fi
106: 
107: if [ ! -r "$MODULE_FILE" ]; then
108:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Module file '$MODULE_FILE' not found or not readable."
109:     exit 1
110: fi
111: 
112: # Calculate Max Concurrency
113: RANGE_SIZE=$((PORT_END - PORT_START + 1))
114: SLOTS=$((RANGE_SIZE / 5))
115: 
116: if [ $SLOTS -lt 1 ]; then
117:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Port range ($PORT_START-$PORT_END) insufficient. Need at least 5 ports."
118:     exit 1
119: fi
120: 
121: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Running systems tests with the following configuration:"
122: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Parallel Slots: $SLOTS (Range: $PORT_START-$PORT_END)"
123: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Running Mode: $RUNNING_MODE"
124: 
125: # Read modules
126: mapfile -t MODULES < <(grep -v '^\s*#' "$MODULE_FILE")
127: 
128: # Check Port Availability (Optimistic check)
129: # Get list of currently used ports once
130: USED_PORTS=$(ss -tuln | awk 'NR>1 {print $5}' | awk -F: '{print $NF}' | sort -u)
131: 
132: for ((i=PORT_START; i<=PORT_END; i++)); do
133:     # Check exact match in used ports list
134:     if echo "$USED_PORTS" | grep -qFx "$i"; then
135:         log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Port $i is already in use. Cannot proceed."
136:         exit 1
137:     fi
138: done
139: 
140: # Pre-build binaries if requested
141: if [ "$DO_BUILD" == "true" ]; then
142:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Pre-building workspace as requested..."
143:     if ! cargo build --workspace; then
144:         log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Pre-build failed."
145:         exit 1
146:     fi
147: else
148:      log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Skipping build step (default). Use --build to enable."
149: fi
150: 
151: # --- Execution Loop ---
152: declare -a PIDS
153: declare -a GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS
154: declare -A PID_PORTS_MAP # PID -> Space separated ports
155: declare -a MODULE_LOGS # PID -> Log file path
156: 
157: cleanup() {
158:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Cleaning up child processes..."
159:     for pid in "${PIDS[@]}"; do
160:         kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null
161:     done
162: }
163: trap cleanup EXIT
164: 
165: process_finished_job() {
166:     local pid=$1
167:     local exit_code=$2
168:     local log_file=${MODULE_LOGS[$pid]}
169:     local ports_str=${PID_PORTS_MAP[$pid]}
170:     
171:     # Check if log file exists before catting
172:     if [ -f "$log_file" ]; then
173:         # Parse results for summary
174:         while read -r line; do
175:             if [[ "$line" == *"[RESULT]"* ]]; then
176:                 # Extract content after [RESULT]
177:                 content=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/.*\[RESULT\] //')
178:                 IFS=':' read -r mod_name test_name status <<< "$content"
179:                 result_modules+=("$mod_name")
180:                 result_test_names+=("$test_name")
181:                 result_statuses+=("$status")
182:                 
183:                 if [[ "$status" == "FAILED" ]]; then
184:                     highest_exit_code=255
185:                 elif [[ "$status" == "SKIPPED" && "$highest_exit_code" -ne 255 ]]; then
186:                     highest_exit_code=77
187:                 elif [[ "$status" == "PASSED" && "$highest_exit_code" -eq 1 ]]; then
188:                     highest_exit_code=0
189:                 fi
190:             else
191:                 echo "$line"
192:             fi
193:         done < "$log_file"
194:         # rm -f "$log_file"
195:     else
196:         log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "error" "Log file for PID $pid not found: $log_file"
197:     fi
198: 
199:     # Release ports
200:     read -r -a ports_to_release <<< "$ports_str"
201:     # Filter GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS to remove ports_to_release
202:     NEW_ALLOC=()
203:     for p in "${GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS[@]}"; do
204:         keep=true
205:         for rel in "${ports_to_release[@]}"; do
206:             if [ "$p" == "$rel" ]; then
207:                 keep=false
208:                 break
209:             fi
210:         done
211:         if [ "$keep" == "true" ]; then
212:             NEW_ALLOC+=("$p")
213:         fi
214:     done
215:     GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS=("${NEW_ALLOC[@]}")
216:     
217:     unset PID_PORTS_MAP[$pid]
218:     unset MODULE_LOGS[$pid]
219:     
220:     # Remove from PIDS array
221:     NEW_PIDS=()
222:     for p in "${PIDS[@]}"; do
223:         if [ "$p" != "$pid" ]; then
224:             NEW_PIDS+=("$p")
225:         fi
226:     done
227:     PIDS=("${NEW_PIDS[@]}")
228: }
229: 
230: # Function to find N free ports
231: find_free_ports() {
232:     local count=$1
233:     local found_ports=()
234:     
235:     # Get list of currently used ports
236:     local used_ports
237:     used_ports=$(ss -tuln | awk '{print $5}' | awk -F: '{print $NF}' | sort -u)
238:     
239:     for ((p=PORT_START; p<=PORT_END; p++)); do
240:         # Check if port is in used_ports
241:         if echo "$used_ports" | grep -q "^$p$"; then
242:             continue
243:         fi
244:         
245:         # Check if port is in GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS
246:         is_allocated=false
247:         for alloc in "${GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS[@]}"; do
248:             if [ "$p" == "$alloc" ]; then
249:                 is_allocated=true
250:                 break
251:             fi
252:         done
253:         
254:         if [ "$is_allocated" == "true" ]; then
255:             continue
256:         fi
257: 
258:         found_ports+=("$p")
259:         
260:         if [ ${#found_ports[@]} -eq "$count" ]; then
261:             echo "${found_ports[@]}"
262:             return 0
263:         fi
264:     done
265:     
266:     # Not enough ports
267:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "debug" "find_free_ports: No ports available in range $PORT_START-$PORT_END"
268:     return 1 
269: }
270: 
271: # Run Modules
272: MODULE_INDEX=0
273: for module_line in "${MODULES[@]}"; do
274:     # Parse module line
275:     read -r -a LINE_PARTS <<< "$module_line"
276:     MODULE_NAME="${LINE_PARTS[0]}"
277:     [ -z "$MODULE_NAME" ] && continue
278:     
279:     # Wait for available ports
280:     while true; do
281:         NEEDED_PORTS=5 # Default requirement
282:         PORTS_STR=$(find_free_ports $NEEDED_PORTS)
283:         
284:         if [ -n "$PORTS_STR" ]; then
285:              log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "debug" "Allocated ports: $PORTS_STR for $MODULE_NAME"
286:              break
287:         fi
288:         
289:         # Wait for something to finish
290:         if [ ${#PIDS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
291:              wait -n -p finished_pid
292:              exit_code=$?
293:              process_finished_job "$finished_pid" "$exit_code"
294:         else
295:              sleep 1
296:         fi
297:     done
298: 
299:     # Launch
300:     read -r -a P_ARRAY <<< "$PORTS_STR"
301:     GLOBAL_ALLOCATED_PORTS+=("${P_ARRAY[@]}")
302:     
303:     SAFE_MODULE_NAME=$(echo "$MODULE_NAME" | tr '/' '_' | tr '.' '_')
304:     TMP_LOG="/tmp/ox_test_${SAFE_MODULE_NAME}_${MODULE_INDEX}.log"
305:     ((MODULE_INDEX++))
306:     
307:     "$SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR/run_module_tests.sh" "$MODULE_NAME" "$PORTS_STR" "$SUPPORT_SCRIPTS_DIR" "$TEST_LIBS_DIR" "$RUNNING_MODE" "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "$TARGET" "$TMP_LOG" &
308:     PID=$!
309:     
310:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "Launched $MODULE_NAME (PID: $PID) with ports: $PORTS_STR"
311:     
312:     PIDS+=("$PID")
313:     PID_PORTS_MAP[$PID]="$PORTS_STR"
314:     MODULE_LOGS[$PID]=$TMP_LOG
315: done
316: 
317: # Wait for remaining jobs
318: for pid in "${PIDS[@]}"; do
319:     if wait "$pid"; then
320:          process_finished_job "$pid" 0
321:     else
322:          process_finished_job "$pid" $?
323:     fi
324: done
325: 
326: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "All modules completed."
327: 
328: # --- Print Grand Summary Table ---
329: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" ""
330: 
331: # --- Calculate dynamic column widths ---
332: # Define Headers
333: MODULE_HEADER="Module"
334: TEST_NAME_HEADER="Test Name"
335: STATUS_HEADER="Status"
336: 
337: # Initialize with header lengths
338: col1_width=${#MODULE_HEADER}
339: col2_width=${#TEST_NAME_HEADER}
340: col3_width=${#STATUS_HEADER}
341: 
342: for i in "${!result_modules[@]}"; do
343:     if (( ${#result_modules[$i]} > col1_width )); then
344:         col1_width=${#result_modules[$i]}
345:     fi
346:     if (( ${#result_test_names[$i]} > col2_width )); then
347:         col2_width=${#result_test_names[$i]}
348:     fi
349:     if (( ${#result_statuses[$i]} > col3_width )); then
350:         col3_width=${#result_statuses[$i]}
351:     fi
352: done
353: 
354: # Add some padding
355: col1_width=${col1_width}
356: col2_width=${col2_width}
357: col3_width=${col3_width}
358: 
359: # ANSI color codes
360: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
361: RED='\033[0;31m'
362: YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
363: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
364: 
365: # Box drawing characters
366: HLINE='-'
367: VLINE='|'
368: CROSS='+'
369: 
370: # Create horizontal line segments
371: hline1=$(printf '%*s' "$((col1_width + 2))" '' | tr ' ' "$HLINE")
372: hline2=$(printf '%*s' "$((col2_width + 2))" '' | tr ' ' "$HLINE")
373: hline3=$(printf '%*s' "$((col3_width + 2))" '' | tr ' ' "$HLINE")
374: 
375: # Calculate total width for the summary header
376: total_width=$((col1_width + col2_width + col3_width + 10))
377: inner_width=$((total_width - 2))
378: summary_hline=$(printf '%*s' "$inner_width" '' | tr ' ' "$HLINE")
379: 
380: # Top border of summary
381: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '%s%s%s' "$CROSS" "$summary_hline" "$CROSS")"
382: 
383: # Summary text
384: summary_text="TESTING SUMMARY"
385: summary_text_len=${#summary_text}
386: padding_total=$((inner_width - summary_text_len))
387: padding_left=$((padding_total / 2))
388: padding_right=$((padding_total - padding_left))
389: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '|%*s%s%*s|' "$padding_left" "" "$summary_text" "$padding_right" "")"
390: 
391: # Top border of table
392: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '%s%s%s%s%s%s%s' "$CROSS" "$hline1" "$CROSS" "$hline2" "$CROSS" "$hline3" "$CROSS")"
393: 
394: # Header
395: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '| %-*s | %-*s | %-*s |' "$col1_width" "$MODULE_HEADER" "$col2_width" "$TEST_NAME_HEADER" "$col3_width" "$STATUS_HEADER")"
396: 
397: # Header separator
398: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '%s%s%s%s%s%s%s' "$CROSS" "$hline1" "$CROSS" "$hline2" "$CROSS" "$hline3" "$CROSS")"
399: 
400: # --- Print table body ---
401: last_module_printed=""
402: for i in "${!result_modules[@]}"; do
403:     MODULE_NAME="${result_modules[$i]}"
404:     TEST_NAME="${result_test_names[$i]}"
405:     STATUS="${result_statuses[$i]}"
406: 
407:     DISPLAY_MODULE_NAME=""
408:     if [ "$MODULE_NAME" != "$last_module_printed" ]; then
409:         DISPLAY_MODULE_NAME=$MODULE_NAME
410:         last_module_printed=$MODULE_NAME
411:     fi
412: 
413:     COLOR=$NC
414:     case "$STATUS" in
415:         "PASSED") COLOR=$GREEN ;;
416:         "FAILED"*) COLOR=$RED ;;
417:         "SKIPPED") COLOR=$YELLOW ;;
418:     esac
419: 
420:     # Format the row with colors, then pass the fully rendered string to log_message
421:     printf -v ROW_STRING "| %-*s | %-*s | %b%-*s%b |" \
422:         "$col1_width" "$DISPLAY_MODULE_NAME" \
423:         "$col2_width" "$TEST_NAME" \
424:         "$COLOR" "$col3_width" "$STATUS" "$NC"
425: 
426:     log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$ROW_STRING"
427: done
428: 
429: # Bottom border
430: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "$(printf '%s%s%s%s%s%s%s' "$CROSS" "$hline1" "$CROSS" "$hline2" "$CROSS" "$hline3" "$CROSS")"
431: 
432: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" ""
433: log_message "$LOGGING_LEVEL" "info" "All specified modules tested."
434: exit $highest_exit_code

(End of file - total 434 lines)
</content>
